name: Sole Suffed with Prawns
recipeInstructions:
1. Melt 2 tablespoons (tbsp) of butter.
2. Add 6 ounces (oz) of shelled prawns, chopped; 4 oz of mushrooms, chopped; 1 oz of dried breadcrumbs; 0.5 teaspoon (ts) of dried dill weed; and 1 ts of salt to the melted butter.
3. Cook the mixture gently for about 5 minutes, stirring occasionally.
4. Spoon some prawn mixture onto each fillet (6 sole fillets, skinned).
5. Roll up each fillet with the prawn mixture inside. Secure with wooden cocktail sticks.
6. Place the rolled fish in a pan with 8 oz of white wine and 4 oz of water. Bring to a boil.
7. Reduce heat and simmer for 12-15 minutes or until cooked. Remove fillets to a warm plate and discard the cocktail sticks.
8. In a separate bowl, blend cornflour and 1 oz of cream.
9. Gradually stir this mixture into the poaching liquid in the pan, stirring constantly.
10. Cook until the sauce boils and thickens. Turn off heat and keep slightly warm.
11. Beat an egg lightly and add a little hot sauce to it.
12. Stir the egg mixture into the sauce continuously until thickened.
13. Spoon some of the sauce over the fish and serve the remaining sauce separately.
